description: Classic four-way Jacoby transfers: 2D and 2H transfer to the majors, 2S transfers to clubs, 2NT transfers to diamonds, with opener accepting at the cheapest step (3C over 2S, 3D over 2NT). Adds side-suit game-forcing rebids, splinter rebids, quantitative 4NT, and super-accept branches on top of the major-suit transfers.
purpose: Cover both major- and minor-suit transfers after 1NT under the classic four-way variant, with cheapest-step minor accepts and structured continuations on top of the standard transfers

authority.label: Bridge Bum — Four Suit Transfers (classic variant: 2S→clubs, 2NT→diamonds)

teaching.tradeoff: Layering new-suit, splinter, quantitative, and super-accept rebids onto the standard transfer structure gives more descriptive rebids at the cost of partnership memory load.
teaching.principle: Keep the transfer principle for right-siding the major-suit contract, then use the second call to describe strength, shape, and slam interest precisely.

commonMistakes.text: Do not transfer with only a four-card major.
commonMistakes.reason: Stayman or natural notrump actions keep the shape description accurate.

commonMistakes.text: Do not forget to continue with invitational or stronger values after opener accepts the transfer.
commonMistakes.reason: Weak hands pass the simple accept, but invitational and stronger hands must rebid to show strength, fit quality, or side-suit plans.

commonMistakes.text: Do not treat this fixture as a minor-suit transfer scheme.
commonMistakes.reason: These surfaces only cover the major-suit routes that start with the standard transfer bids.

explanationEntries.displayText: Eligible for Jacoby Transfer
Transfer is the preferred convention
Transfer target suit determined by longest major
Opener has 3+ hearts to fit responder's suit
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Opener lacks heart fit (fewer than 3 hearts)

explanationEntries.displayText: Opener has 3+ spades to fit responder's suit
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Opener lacks spade fit (fewer than 3 spades)

explanationEntries.displayText: Transfer to hearts: bid 2♦ to show 5+ hearts
Transfer to spades: bid 2♥ to show 5+ spades
Accept transfer: bid the target major (hearts)
Accept transfer: bid the target major (spades)
Sign off in hearts: weak hand, pass and play in the major
Raise to 4♥: game values with 5+ hearts
Bid 3NT: game values, offers opener choice between 3NT and 4♥
Raise to 3♥: invitational with 6+ hearts, opener decides game
Bid 2NT: invitational with exactly 5 hearts, opener decides
Bid 2♠: invitational with 5 hearts and 5 spades, non-forcing
Bid 3♣: game forcing with 5+ hearts and 4+ clubs
Bid 3♦: game forcing with 5+ hearts and 4+ diamonds
Bid 3♠: singleton/void in spades with slam interest
Bid 4NT: quantitative slam invite in hearts or notrump
Sign off in spades: weak hand, pass and play in the major
Raise to 4♠: game values with 5+ spades
Bid 3NT: game values, offers opener choice between 3NT and 4♠
Raise to 3♠: invitational with 6+ spades, opener decides game
Bid 2NT: invitational with exactly 5 spades, opener decides
Bid 3♣: game forcing with 5+ spades and 4+ clubs
Bid 3♦: game forcing with 5+ spades and 4+ diamonds
Bid 3♥: 5-5 majors with slam interest (stronger than 4♥)
Bid 4♥: game values with 5+ spades and 5+ hearts
Bid 4NT: quantitative slam invite in spades or notrump
Correct to 4♥: opener has 3+ hearts, prefer the major game
Pass 3NT: opener lacks heart fit, stay in notrump
Correct to 4♠: opener has 3+ spades, prefer the major game
Pass 3NT: opener lacks spade fit, stay in notrump
Accept heart invite: bid 4♥ with maximum and heart fit
Accept heart invite: bid 3NT with maximum but no heart fit
Sign off in 3♥: minimum hand but 3+ hearts (fit found)
Decline heart invite: pass 2NT with minimum and no heart fit
Accept spade invite: bid 4♠ with maximum and spade fit
Accept spade invite: bid 3NT with maximum but no spade fit
Sign off in 3♠: minimum hand but 3+ spades (fit found)
Decline spade invite: pass 2NT with minimum and no spade fit
Accept heart invite raise: bid 4♥ with maximum, fit and extras
Decline heart invite raise: pass 3♥ with minimum
Accept spade invite raise: bid 4♠ with maximum, fit and extras
Decline spade invite raise: pass 3♠ with minimum
Transfer to clubs: bid 2♠ to show 6+ clubs (weak or invitational)
Transfer to diamonds: bid 2NT to show 6+ diamonds (weak or invitational)
Accept minor transfer: bid 3♣ at the cheapest step
Accept minor transfer: bid 3♦ at the cheapest step
Pass 3♣: sign off in the long minor with a weak hand
Pass 3♦: sign off in the long minor with a weak hand
Bid 3NT: game values, offers opener choice between 3NT and 5♣
Bid 3NT: game values, offers opener choice between 3NT and 5♦
Raise to 4♣: slam try with long clubs
Raise to 4♦: slam try with long diamonds
Jump to 3♥: super-accept with a maximum and 4-card heart support
Jump to 3♠: super-accept with a maximum and 4-card spade support

teachingLabel.name: Transfer to hearts
teachingLabel.summary: Bid 2D to ask opener to bid 2H, keeping the strong hand as declarer

teachingLabel.name: Transfer to spades
teachingLabel.summary: Bid 2H to ask opener to bid 2S, keeping the strong hand as declarer

clauses.rationale: hands with game-forcing values and a long minor prefer a direct game-forcing route; the minor transfer is a weak-or-invitational rebid platform

teachingLabel.name: Transfer to clubs
teachingLabel.summary: Bid two spades to ask opener to bid three clubs, right-siding a long club suit

clauses.rationale: hands with game-forcing values and a long minor prefer a direct game-forcing route; the minor transfer is a weak-or-invitational rebid platform

teachingLabel.name: Transfer to diamonds
teachingLabel.summary: Bid two notrump to ask opener to bid three diamonds, right-siding a long diamond suit

teachingLabel.name: Accept transfer to clubs
teachingLabel.summary: Bid three clubs at the cheapest step as requested by the minor transfer

teachingLabel.name: Accept transfer to diamonds
teachingLabel.summary: Bid three diamonds at the cheapest step as requested by the minor transfer

clauses.rationale: weak single-suited hand wants to play in the transfer suit at the cheapest level
the transfer promised a long club suit, confirmed here for the signoff

teachingLabel.name: Signoff in clubs
teachingLabel.summary: Pass 3C with a weak single-suited hand to play in the safer minor-suit partscore

clauses.rationale: long minor that can supply tricks in notrump

clauses.description: No short suit (balanced enough for NT; stoppers expected in unbid suits)
clauses.rationale: without an authored stopper fact in the catalog, balanced shape stands in as a teaching proxy for stopper expectations in the unbid suits

clauses.description: No slam values (game values, not slam)
clauses.rationale: slam-interested hands continue with 4C rather than signing off in 3NT

teachingLabel.name: 3NT choice of games
teachingLabel.summary: Offer opener a choice between 3NT and 5C with a long running club suit and balanced values

clauses.rationale: slam values with a long minor and club fit agreed
long trump source for a minor-suit slam

teachingLabel.summary: Raise to 4C with slam interest, inviting opener to cooperate via keycard or cue bids

summaryCard.trigger: Partner opens one notrump and responder transfers to either a long major or a long minor
summaryCard.partnership: Classic four-way transfer agreement covering both majors and minors. Majors follow the standard signoff, invite, notrump-choice, and game ladder plus super-accepts; minors accept at the cheapest step and carry a compact responder-rebid menu: pass to sign off in the minor, bid notrump to offer a choice of games, or raise the minor as a slam try. Deferred scope: break and decline variants on the minor side, minor splinters, and the higher choice-of-slam rebid.

whenToUse.gloss: Use a transfer with a five-card or longer heart suit.
Use a transfer with a five-card or longer spade suit.
Transfer to clubs with a six-card or longer suit and weak-or-invitational values.
Transfer to diamonds with a six-card or longer suit and weak-or-invitational values.

workedAuctions.label: Weak heart hand signs off safely

calls.rationale: Opener begins with the balanced notrump opening.
Responder transfers to hearts with a long suit and a weak hand.
Opener completes the transfer as requested.
Responder signs off because the transfer already found the best resting spot.

workedAuctions.label: Spade invite keeps opener in front

calls.rationale: Opener shows the balanced notrump range.
Responder transfers into spades before showing invitational strength.
Opener accepts the transfer.
Responder invites game with the follow-up rebid instead of guessing immediately.
With extra values and a fit, opener accepts the invite in the major from the strong side.

workedAuctions.label: Balanced heart game offers opener a choice

calls.rationale: Opener starts with the balanced notrump opening.
Responder transfers to hearts before showing game values.
Opener completes the transfer.
With exactly five balanced hearts, responder lets opener choose between notrump and the fit.
Opener corrects to the major with a heart fit, still declaring from the stronger hand.

workedAuctions.label: Counter-example: a four-card major is not a transfer hand

calls.rationale: Opener starts with the balanced notrump opening.
Responder wrongly transfers as though the spade suit were long enough.
Opener accepts the transfer in routine fashion.
The auction now overstates responder's spade length and lands in the wrong strain.

workedAuctions.label: Weak club hand transfers and signs off

calls.rationale: Opener begins with the balanced notrump opening.
Responder bids 2♠ to transfer to clubs with a long, weak single-suited hand.
Opener completes the transfer at the cheapest step.
Responder passes 3♣ to rest in the long minor.

workedAuctions.label: Long diamond hand offers choice of games

calls.rationale: Opener begins with the balanced notrump opening.
Responder bids 2NT to transfer to diamonds with a long, running minor and game values.
Opener accepts the diamond transfer at the cheapest step.
With the minor suit shown and balanced values, responder offers a choice between 3NT and 5♦.

items.ourAction: Some partnerships keep transfers on while others switch to rescue methods.
items.note: Confirm the agreement before assuming the transfer survives the double.

items.ourAction: Transfer structure is often replaced by cue bids or natural competition.
items.note: If the partnership does not keep systems on, respond naturally and preserve space.

items.ourAction: The transfer room is gone, so compete naturally or use agreed rescue methods.
items.note: Once the opponents consume the transfer step, the artificial meaning disappears.

relatedLinks.discriminator: Standard Jacoby Transfers cover the core transfer calls and the signoff / invite / game ladder; this page adds splinter, new-suit game-force, quantitative, and super-accept rebids on top of that foundation.
Stayman handles hands built around a four-card major rather than a long transfer suit.
Smolen starts with Stayman when both majors matter and game is forced; use Smolen rather than these extended transfer rebids when responder has five-four majors.

definitions.description: Transfer target suit (hearts, spades, or none)
Eligible for Jacoby transfer (5+ card major)
Opener has 3+ hearts (fit with responder's 5-card suit)
Opener has 3+ spades (fit with responder's 5-card suit)
